stories.title: New Flight Makes Sarawak Just a Hop Away!
stories.headline_kid: A brand-new airline just launched its very first flight, connecting Singapore straight to Kuching in Sarawak!
summary: A new airline called AirBorneo has started flying between Singapore and Kuching, the capital of Sarawak in Malaysia. Its very first plane landed at Changi Airport's Terminal 4 on 22 July, and the airline chose that date on purpose - it was Sarawak Day, a special day celebrating the state.

Sarawak is famous for its thick rainforests, orangutans and traditional longhouses, so this flight makes it much easier for Singapore families to visit for a holiday. Tickets went on sale on 13 July, and already about 80 per cent of seats for next year's Chinese New Year period are booked!

AirBorneo is owned by the Sarawak state government, and this Singapore route is its very first international flight ever. The airline is excited about the strong demand and now hopes to add more flights to other cities across the ASEAN region.

This new route shows how Singapore's Changi Airport keeps growing as a hub, connecting our little island to more and more places nearby. It's good news for anyone who dreams of seeing rainforests and wildlife just a short flight away!

lenses.me: If you love animals like orangutans, this new flight means a rainforest adventure could be just a short plane ride away!
lenses.family: Your family might now consider Sarawak for the next school holiday trip, especially since Chinese New Year seats are already filling up fast.
lenses.friends: You and your classmates might swap stories about visiting Borneo's rainforests during the next long break.
lenses.nation: It shows Singapore's Changi Airport is becoming even more connected, welcoming new airlines and new destinations.
lenses.world: It strengthens friendly ties between Singapore and different parts of Malaysia, not just Kuala Lumpur.

compo_words.meaning: the first of something, marking a special beginning
compo_words.example: The inaugural flight from Kuching landed at Changi Airport to loud cheers.
The demand for tickets was so high that most seats were sold within weeks.

compo_words.meaning: a path or way travelled from one place to another
compo_words.example: The airline plans to open more routes across Southeast Asia.

compo_phrases.example: Tickets for the Chinese New Year flights were in high demand.
The airline eyes expansion into more ASEAN countries next year.

questions: Have you ever travelled by plane to visit family or go on holiday? Describe your experience.
If you could choose any new flight route to open, where would you want it to go and why?
Why do you think it is exciting when a new airline starts flying to a new place?

In my opinion, travelling helps us...
One thing I would like to see is...
This reminds me of a time when...
I think this is important because...

sources.name: CNA - AirBorneo eyes ASEAN expansion after Singapore-Sarawak route launch

stories.title: Singapore's Giant Piggy Bank Grew a Little Slower
stories.headline_kid: GIC, the fund that invests Singapore's savings around the world, made less extra money this year than usual.
summary: GIC is like a giant piggy bank that manages Singapore's national savings, called reserves. Instead of just keeping the money in a safe, GIC invests it in companies and projects all over the world, hoping it will grow bigger over time.

GIC announced that its 20-year average return, after accounting for rising prices (called inflation), is now 3.4 per cent - the lowest it has been in six years. This means the extra money GIC earned grew a bit more slowly compared to before.

GIC said it chose to take less risk this year, meaning it played it safer with its investments instead of chasing big but risky gains. This is because the world economy has been shaky, with trade tensions and worries about whether AI companies are overvalued.

Even though the number is lower, GIC's job is to think long-term - like saving steadily for many years rather than betting everything on one risky idea. The money GIC grows helps fund important things in Singapore, like healthcare and support for older residents.

lenses.me: It won't change your pocket money, but it shows how careful planning with money matters, even for a whole country.
lenses.family: Some government support schemes your family benefits from are partly funded by returns like these.
lenses.friends: You could discuss with friends why saving steadily is often smarter than risky quick wins.
lenses.nation: It shows how Singapore carefully protects its reserves instead of gambling them away, keeping the country financially strong.
lenses.world: It reflects how uncertain the global economy has been, affecting investors everywhere, not just in Singapore.

compo_words.meaning: the ability to stay strong and recover from difficulties
compo_words.example: GIC prioritised resilience over chasing risky, quick profits.

compo_words.meaning: to treat something as more important than other things
compo_words.example: The fund decided to prioritise safety during uncertain times.

compo_phrases.meaning: acted carefully to avoid big risks
compo_phrases.example: GIC took a cautious approach by investing more safely this year.
Careful saving helps a country weather economic storms.

questions: Do you have a piggy bank or savings account? What do you save your money for?
Why do you think it might be wise to save carefully instead of taking big risks?
How do you think a country's savings help ordinary families like yours?
phrases: I believe saving money is important because...
This reminds me of how I save my own pocket money...
I think being careful with money means...
In the long run, I feel that...
This is similar to when my family...

sources.name: CNA - GIC's 20-year annualised real return falls to 3.4%, lowest in 6 years

stories.title: Japan's Scorching Heat Sends Hundreds to Hospital
stories.headline_kid: Japan is so hot right now that 453 people had to go to hospital in just one day!
summary: Japan is experiencing a serious heatwave, and the Tokyo Fire Department said 453 people needed hospital treatment for heat-related problems on a single day this week. That's the highest number since they started keeping records 16 years ago!

Doctors call this danger heatstroke - when your body gets so hot it cannot cool itself down, which can make people feel dizzy, faint or even seriously ill. Sadly, at least 14 people have died across Japan from the heat over the past week.

This matters for Singapore families too, especially if you're planning a trip to Japan during the school holidays. Even though Singapore is hot all year round, Japan's summer heat can catch visitors by surprise because it's usually cooler there.

Experts say heatwaves like this are becoming more common and more extreme because of climate change. If you or your family are travelling somewhere hot, remember to drink lots of water, wear a hat, and avoid staying out in direct sun for too long.

lenses.me: If you're travelling to Japan this holiday, remember to carry water and take breaks in the shade.
lenses.family: Parents planning an overseas trip should check the weather forecast and pack sun protection for everyone.
lenses.friends: You could remind friends going on school trips abroad to stay hydrated and watch out for heat warning signs.
lenses.nation: It's a reminder that even Singapore, which is already hot, must stay alert to heat-related health risks.
lenses.world: It shows how climate change is making heatwaves more frequent and dangerous around the world, not just in Asia.

compo_words.example: Tourists struggled in the sweltering heat of Tokyo's summer.

compo_words.meaning: a dangerous condition when the body overheats
compo_words.example: He was rushed to hospital after suffering from heatstroke.
The number of hospital cases was unprecedented in 16 years.

compo_phrases.example: People sweltered under the sun as temperatures soared.

compo_phrases.meaning: go to a doctor or hospital for help
compo_phrases.example: Hundreds sought medical treatment after collapsing in the heat.

questions: Have you ever felt very tired or dizzy because of hot weather? What did you do?
What are some ways you stay cool and safe when the weather is very hot?
Why do you think it's important to check the weather before travelling to another country?

sources.name: CNA - Hundreds hospitalised in Japan heatwave 'disaster'

stories.title: Sad News: Attack in Southern Thailand Leaves Soldiers Dead
stories.headline_kid: An attack in southern Thailand has killed five soldiers, reminding travellers to always check safety news before a trip.
summary: In southern Thailand, near the Narathiwat province, attackers used a bomb and gunfire to hit a checkpoint, sadly killing five soldiers and injuring six other people. Thailand's Prime Minister called it a serious and shocking act of violence.

This part of southern Thailand has faced unrest for many years. A small group there disagrees with the government and sometimes uses violence to make their point - this is called an insurgency, which simply means an ongoing conflict within a country.

This news matters to Singapore because many Singaporean families travel to southern Thailand, especially places like Hat Yai, for holidays and shopping trips. Whenever there is unrest in a travel destination, Singapore's government usually issues travel advisories - official warnings to help keep our citizens safe.

It's a gentle reminder that before any trip abroad, it helps to check the news and any safety advice from trusted sources like Singapore's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, so families can make informed decisions about where and when to travel.

lenses.me: It reminds you that it's always smart to listen to your parents and teachers about travel safety.
lenses.family: If your family is planning a trip to southern Thailand, it's worth checking for the latest travel advisories first.
lenses.friends: You could discuss with classmates why some travel destinations sometimes come with safety warnings.
lenses.nation: Singapore's government keeps a close watch on regional safety to protect citizens travelling abroad.
lenses.world: It shows that conflicts in one part of a country can still affect visitors and neighbouring nations.

compo_words.meaning: an ongoing armed rebellion against a government
compo_words.example: The region has faced a long-running insurgency for many years.

compo_words.meaning: a place where security guards check people or vehicles
compo_words.example: Soldiers were stationed at the checkpoint to keep the area safe.

compo_words.meaning: to strongly criticise something as wrong
compo_words.example: The Prime Minister condemned the attack as a serious threat.

compo_phrases.meaning: a bold and shocking action done without shame
compo_phrases.example: The government called the bombing a brazen act of violence.

compo_phrases.meaning: extra safety measures put in place
compo_phrases.example: Heightened security was ordered across the region after the attack.

questions: Why do you think it's important to check safety news before travelling somewhere new?
How would you feel if your family's holiday plans had to change because of a safety warning?

sources.name: CNA - Five soldiers killed in bomb attack in Thailand's restive south

stories.title: The Secret Boot City That Singaporeans Love to Visit
stories.headline_kid: A cool mountain city in Indonesia called Bandung is famous for something surprising - amazing handmade boots!
summary: Bandung is a city in Indonesia known for its cool mountain air, tea plantations and old buildings. But among people who love shoes, Bandung has a special secret - it's becoming world-famous for handmade boots crafted with great skill and care.

Brands like Sagara Bootmaker, Briselblack, Onderhoud Handmade and Txture have earned fans not just in Indonesia, but from all around the world. Each boot is carefully made by skilled craftsmen, called artisans, using techniques that take years to master.

Bandung is a popular weekend getaway for many Singaporeans, who enjoy the cooler weather and often go shopping for well-made, affordable items - including these boots. Compared to famous Western boot brands, Bandung's handmade boots offer similar quality at a friendlier price.

This story shows how a small city can become known worldwide for one special skill, and it celebrates the patience and care that goes into making something by hand - a good reminder that handmade items often carry a lot of heart and hard work.

lenses.me: It might inspire you to try a hands-on hobby, like crafting or drawing, where you make something with care.
lenses.family: Your family might consider Bandung for a fun, budget-friendly weekend trip with cooler weather.
lenses.friends: You could compare handmade versus shop-bought items with friends for a fun class discussion or project.
lenses.nation: It shows Singapore's close and friendly travel ties with nearby Indonesian cities beyond just Bali or Jakarta.
lenses.world: It highlights how small cities can build a big global reputation through skill and craftsmanship.

compo_words.meaning: skill in making things by hand
compo_words.example: The boots are famous for their excellent craftsmanship.

compo_words.meaning: a skilled worker who makes things by hand
compo_words.example: Each artisan spends weeks perfecting a single pair of boots.
The city is renowned for its beautiful mountain scenery.

compo_phrases.example: The bootmaker earned a following among collectors worldwide.

compo_phrases.meaning: something wonderful that not many people know about yet
compo_phrases.example: Bandung is considered the world's best-kept secret for handmade boots.
